message.text: Step Function state machine this: no logging_configuration. Execution history (state transitions, input/output data, errors) isn't emitted to CloudWatch Logs — forensic investigation post-failure is impossible.
Step Function state machine this: tracing_configuration.enabled = false. Distributed traces across the Lambdas / SQS queues / DDB tables this machine orchestrates aren't captured.

SQS queue queue: no redrive_policy (no dead-letter queue) and no kms_master_key_id. Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)
SQS queue queue: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.

SQS queue queue: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.
Log group external: retention_in_days unset. Default is `Never Expire`. Logs accumulate at storage rates indefinitely; the only way out is a runbook to manually purge.

CloudWatch Log Group external: no retention_in_days set. Logs are kept forever — at $0.03/GB/month, a moderately-active service generating 1 GB/day hits ~$900/month in log storage alone after 12 months.
this: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.

CloudWatch log group external: no retention_in_days is set. Logs accumulate indefinitely — CloudWatch Logs storage is $0.03/GB/month with no automatic pruning. A single high-volume application (e.g., ECS service, Lambda) can silently accumulate hundreds of GB over months.
AWS provider: no default_tags block. Every resource has to tag itself for Cost Explorer — teams forget on a third of them.

Log group (external) has no retention_in_days set. CloudWatch keeps logs forever by default at $0.03/GB-month. A high-traffic service generating 10 GB/day accumulates ~$110/month in storage after one year with no bound in sight.

fullDescription.text: Log group (external) has no retention_in_days set. CloudWatch keeps logs forever by default at $0.03/GB-month. A high-traffic service generating 10 GB/day accumulates ~$110/month in storage after one year with no bound in sight.
